title: vangueria randii subsp. acuminata
intro: Vangueria randii subsp. acuminata, also known as the "wild guelder rose" or "kayombo," is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vangueria randii subsp. acuminata include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used as a poultice to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
